channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: 40-year-old Cape Verde goalkeeper keeps favorite Spain to 0 goals at World Cup
item.description: Vozinha recorded seven saves Monday, holding Spain's star-studded lineup to a shocking 0-0 draw. The veteran keeper was everywhere as the Spanish team and its fans became increasingly frustrated.
item.content:encoded:
Vozinha recorded seven saves Monday, holding Spain's star-studded lineup to a shocking 0-0 draw. The veteran keeper was everywhere as the Spanish team and its fans became increasingly frustrated.

(Image credit: Mike Stewart)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: UFC boss Dana White says 'never again' to another White House fight night
item.description: The headaches over weather concerns in the outdoors show, the logistics of construction of the cage and staging events at federal landmarks and the soaring cost made Freedom 250 a one-off.
item.content:encoded:
The headaches over weather concerns in the outdoors show, the logistics of construction of the cage and staging events at federal landmarks and the soaring cost made Freedom 250 a one-off.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: G7 leaders open summit talks in France on Ukraine and the Middle East
item.description: The talks come on the heels of President Trump's announcement of an agreement to end the U.S. war against Iran. In recent weeks, the Iranian conflict has overshadowed the war in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
The talks come on the heels of President Trump's announcement of an agreement to end the U.S. war against Iran. In recent weeks, the Iranian conflict has overshadowed the war in Ukraine.

(Image credit: Christopher Katsarov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Former ICE leader lands new job consulting on national security and defense
item.description: Because of his former job, Todd Lyons cannot engage with the Department of Homeland Security for a year, per federal law.
item.content:encoded:
Because of his former job, Todd Lyons cannot engage with the Department of Homeland Security for a year, per federal law.

(Image credit: Samuel Corum)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: 'Sometimes splitting is a good thing': An Illinois movement aims to be the 51st state
item.description: A third of Illinois counties have passed resolutions seeking to separate from the city of Chicago. People in these rural counties resent what they see as disproportionate power wielded by Chicago.
item.content:encoded: A third of Illinois counties have passed resolutions seeking to separate from the city of Chicago. People in these rural counties resent what they see as disproportionate power wielded by Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Connor O'Neill

item.title: Snail Mail on her latest album, 'Ricochet'
item.description: Lindsey Jordan, of Snail Mail, tackles mortality, religion and the afterlife on her third studio album.
item.content:encoded:
Lindsey Jordan, of Snail Mail, tackles mortality, religion and the afterlife on her third studio album.

(Image credit: Daria Kobayashi Ritch)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Kallao

item.title: Abdullah Ibrahim, quiet giant of the jazz piano, has died at 91
item.description: The South African musician's "Mannenberg" was often called his country's unofficial anthem during the final years of apartheid.
item.content:encoded:
The South African musician's "Mannenberg" was often called his country's unofficial anthem during the final years of apartheid.

(Image credit: Mujahid Safodien)
item.dc:creator: Martin Johnson

item.title: As the U.S. turns 250, this historian has blunt advice: 'America has to grow up'
item.description: In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.
item.content:encoded:
In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.

(Image credit: Lisa Lake)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
item.description: Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.
item.content:encoded:
Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.

(Image credit: Gemunu Amarasinghe)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: As Luigi Mangione's lawyers head to court, support grows for the accused 'vigilante'
item.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team is back in court in New York City this week for a key pretrial hearing. He's accused of stalking and killing an insurance CEO. Donors have given $1.5 million to support his defense.
item.content:encoded:
Luigi Mangione's legal team is back in court in New York City this week for a key pretrial hearing. He's accused of stalking and killing an insurance CEO. Donors have given $1.5 million to support his defense.

(Image credit: Leon Neal)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
